name: Prose: "Empowered Women Navigating the World"

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - two dedicated MCQ sets (12 questions total), Group 2/2A specific

researchNotes: OCR index search for 'navika sagar parikrama/insv tarini/empowered women' returned zero hits — web research only. This is a non-fiction informational prose (a real news-based account of the Indian Navy's all-women circumnavigation voyage), unlike most other Unit VII pieces which are short stories/poems — expect more FACT-recall style questions (numbers, names, places) than figure-of-speech ones.

reading.intro: A real, recent (2017-18) non-fiction account of 'Navika Sagar Parikrama' — the Indian Navy's first all-women crew to circumnavigate the globe non-stop, aboard the indigenously-built sailboat INSV Tarini, showcasing women's empowerment and the 'Make in India' initiative.

qa.question: What does INSV stand for, what kind of vessel is INSV Tarini, and after whom is it named?
qa.answer: INSV = Indian Naval Ship Vessel. INSV Tarini is a 55-foot sailboat. It is named after Tara-Tarini, regarded as the patron deity for sailors.

qa.question: How many crew members undertook the voyage, how long did the voyage last, and what renewable energy source did the vessel use?
qa.answer: Six all-women crew members. The voyage lasted 254 days. The vessel used wind energy (it is a sailboat).

qa.question: Where and how long were the crew trained, and who mentored them?
qa.answer: They trained in Mumbai and Kochi, over a period of 3 years. They were mentored by Commander Dilip Donde (himself the first Indian to complete a solo non-stop circumnavigation).

qa.question: What was the name of the overall project/mission, and what national initiative did it aim to showcase?
qa.answer: The mission was called 'Navika Sagar Parikrama'. It aimed to showcase women's empowerment and the indigenous 'Make in India' initiative (since INSV Tarini was built in India).

qa.question: What quality of the skipper was credited with the expedition's success, and what value did one crew member (Payal Gupta) particularly highlight?
qa.answer: Honesty was credited as the skipper's key quality for successful leadership. Payal Gupta spoke about the importance of teamwork during the voyage.

qa.question: What festival did the crew celebrate while at sea, illustrating their resilience and team spirit far from home?
